questions:
🎣 Hook, Line & Thinker

You pull up to a lake you have never fished. Water is stained, no visible cover. Where do you start and why? 👇

🎣 Hook, Line & Thinker

Shad are schooling on the surface but your topwater is getting zero reaction. You have got 15 minutes to figure it out — what is your diagnostic move? 👇

🎣 Hook, Line & Thinker

You catch a solid fish on a dropshot and notice its belly is packed with crawfish. What does that tell you about the forage shift happening right now? 👇

🎣 Hook, Line & Thinker

It is 5:45 PM, water is glassy calm, sun is dropping fast. You have got 20 minutes before dark on a dock-lined reservoir. What is your last-ditch setup? 👇

🎣 Hook, Line & Thinker

You lost the same fish twice on the same spot in 10 minutes — both times the hook pulled. Same spot, same lure. What is likely wrong and what is your fix? 👇

🎣 Hook, Line & Thinker

Same body of water, same day. You caught them shallow in the morning off beds. Where are you fishing at 2 PM and why? 👇

🎣 Hook, Line & Thinker

Your Texas-rigged senko gets follows but zero bites. You know fish are there because you can see them. What is the most likely fix without switching lures? 👇

🎣 Hook, Line & Thinker

Water temperature reads 68°F at 8 AM. Spring pattern or full summer mode — and where are you fishing? 👇

🎣 This Week's Pattern

Post-spawn bass are pulling back to main lake points and rocky banks. A 3/8oz rattle trap fished 2–3ft below the surface has been money.

🎣 Water's Warming Up

Bass are moving off beds and into cane fields, lily pads, and docks with shade. The window between 9 AM and noon is your friend right now.

🎣 Gear Note

Fluorocarbon mainline is showing better bite sensitivity as water clarity improves — especially on finesse applications like Neko rigs and Ned rigs.

🎣 Baitfish Update

Shad are thick in the system right now. If you are not throwing some form of shad-colored profile, you might be leaving bites on the table.

🎣 Pattern Worth Trying

Wacky rigging a senko on a light wire hook with no weight — let it fall natural in 10–15ft of water around creek channel bends.

🎣 Seasonal Transition Tip

Bass are moving off the bank but not yet in their summer deep-water pattern. The window between spawn and summer depth is one of the best bites of the year.

🎣 Topwater Season Starting

Evening topwater on frog baits is starting to fire on calm days. Do not leave the water before dark — that is when the magic happens.

🎣 Early Season Reminder

Check your drag settings after a few months off. Nothing kills a big fish day faster than a frozen drag on a hard-pulling bass.
